description: Fix CLI user experience issues: unhandled audit error, status formatting, plugin remove false positive, config set browser-tool naming friction, config set tool. port resolution, and config set port restart message

userStories.title: Handle invalid --since duration gracefully in audit command
userStories.description: As a user, I want a clean error message when I provide an invalid --since value to opentabs audit, so that I understand what format is expected without seeing a stack trace
acceptanceCriteria: Running `opentabs audit --since invalid` prints a user-friendly error message (not a stack trace) and exits with code 1
Running `opentabs audit --file --since invalid` also prints a clean error and exits with code 1
Valid --since values (e.g., 30m, 1h, 2d) continue to work correctly
The error message includes the invalid value and the expected format (e.g., 30m, 1h, 2d)
userStories.notes:
File: platform/cli/src/commands/audit.ts

The `parseDuration` function (lines 52-59) throws a Commander `InvalidArgumentError` when the format doesn't match `^(\d+)([smhd])$`. This is called at runtime inside `handleAudit` (line 204) and `handleAuditFromFile` (line 125), NOT during Commander's option parsing phase, so Commander doesn't catch it.

Fix: Wrap the `parseDuration(options.since)` calls in both `handleAudit` (line 204) and `handleAuditFromFile` (line 125) in a try/catch. On `InvalidArgumentError`, print the error cleanly using `console.error(pc.red(...))` and `process.exit(1)`. Re-throw any other error types.

Alternative cleaner approach: change `parseDuration` to NOT throw — instead return `null` on invalid input. Then the callers check for null and print the error. This avoids try/catch boilerplate. Either approach is fine.

userStories.title: Fix 'Plugin reloads' label padding in status output
userStories.description: As a user, I want the status output to be properly aligned so that all labels and values are consistently formatted
acceptanceCriteria: Running `opentabs status` shows a space between 'Plugin reloads' and its value (e.g., 'Plugin reloads  0' not 'Plugin reloads0')
All other status labels remain properly aligned
userStories.notes:
File: platform/cli/src/commands/status.ts

Line 30: `const pad = (label: string) => \`  ${pc.cyan(label.padEnd(14))}\`;`
Line 104: `console.log(\`${pad('Plugin reloads')}${String(data.reloadCount)}\`);`

'Plugin reloads' is exactly 14 characters, so `padEnd(14)` adds zero padding. Fix: change `padEnd(14)` to `padEnd(16)` on line 30. This gives 2 spaces of gap for the longest label and more for shorter ones. All other labels are shorter than 14 chars so they'll just get slightly more padding, which is fine.

userStories.title: Warn when plugin remove targets a plugin that is not installed
userStories.description: As a user, I want opentabs plugin remove to tell me when the plugin I'm trying to remove is not installed, so that I don't get a misleading 'Successfully removed' message
acceptanceCriteria: Running `opentabs plugin remove nonexistent-plugin --confirm` prints an error like 'Plugin <name> is not installed globally' and exits with code 1
Running `opentabs plugin remove slack --confirm` when slack IS installed succeeds as before
The check uses `npm list -g <pkg> --depth=0` or equivalent to verify installation before attempting removal

userStories.title: Auto-prefix browser tool shorthand names in config set
userStories.description: As a user, I want to type `opentabs config set browser-tool.screenshot_tab disabled` without the redundant `browser_` prefix, so that the config key naming is intuitive
acceptanceCriteria: `opentabs config set browser-tool.screenshot_tab disabled` works (auto-prefixes to `browser_screenshot_tab`)
`opentabs config set browser-tool.browser_screenshot_tab disabled` still works (already prefixed)
`opentabs config set browser-tool.extension_id enabled` still works (extension_ prefix)
The error message for empty names still works correctly

userStories.title: Pass port option through to handleListTools in config set
userStories.description: As a user, I want `opentabs config set tool.` to respect my --port flag and configured port, so that it can reach the server when it's running on a non-default port
acceptanceCriteria: `opentabs config set tool. --port 8080` queries the server on port 8080 for available tools
`OPENTABS_PORT=8080 opentabs config set tool.` queries the server on port 8080
Default port (9515) still works when no port is specified
userStories.notes:
File: platform/cli/src/commands/config.ts

The issue is in `handleConfigSet` (around line 394-396). When `key === TOOL_PREFIX`, it calls `handleListTools()` with no arguments:
```typescript
if (key === TOOL_PREFIX) {
  return handleListTools();
}
```

But `options` (which contains the parsed `--port`) is available in `handleConfigSet`'s parameters.

Fix:
1. Change `handleListTools` signature (line 174) from `async (): Promise<void>` to `async (options: { port?: number }): Promise<void>`
2. Change line 175 from `resolvePort({})` to `resolvePort(options)`
3. Change the call site (line 396) from `return handleListTools();` to `return handleListTools(options);`

userStories.title: Show restart-required message when changing port via config set
userStories.description: As a user, I want to be told that a server restart is required after changing the port, so that I don't assume the change takes effect immediately
acceptanceCriteria: `opentabs config set port 8080` prints the new port value AND a message indicating a restart is needed (e.g., 'Restart the MCP server for the port change to take effect.')
The restart message is visually distinct (e.g., yellow/dim) from the confirmation
The `notifyServer` call is either removed (since port changes can't take effect via reload) or kept but the restart message is still shown
userStories.notes:
File: platform/cli/src/commands/config.ts

Function `handleSetPort` (lines 274-288). Currently line 286 prints `port: <value>` and line 287 calls `notifyServer`.

Fix: After line 286 (`console.log(\`port: ${pc.cyan(String(newPort))}\`);`), add:
```typescript
console.log(pc.yellow('Restart the MCP server for the port change to take effect.'));
```

The `notifyServer` call on line 287 can be kept (it triggers a config reload on the server which picks up other config changes), but the restart message must be prominent. Alternatively, remove the `notifyServer` call since the server can't rebind its port without restarting — keeping it is harmless but slightly misleading.
